ogTitle: Budgewoi Peninsula vs Buff Point: Climate Safety

heading: Budgewoi Peninsula vs Buff Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Budgewoi Peninsula, New South Wales and Buff Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Budgewoi Peninsula has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Buff Point (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Budgewoi Peninsula or Buff Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Budgewoi Peninsula has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Buff Point scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Budgewoi Peninsula scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Buff Point scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Budgewoi Peninsula or Buff Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Budgewoi Peninsula has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
